title: Novavax Q1 2024 Financial Results: Game-Changing Sanofi Partnership and Removal of Going Concern
text: Novavax entered into a co-exclusive licensing agreement with Sanofi representing a potential multi-billion dollar revenue opportunity. The deal includes a $500 million upfront payment and approximately $70 million equity investment in Novavax stock. Additional milestones could reach up to $700 million for COVID-19 products and $200 million per new vaccine using Novavax's Matrix-M adjuvant. Q1 2024 generated $94 million in total revenue, up from $81 million in Q1 2023. However, the company recorded a net loss of $148 million compared to $294 million in the prior year period. Research and development expenses declined significantly to $93 million from $247 million, while selling, general and administrative costs dropped to $87 million from $113 million. The company advanced its updated COVID-19 vaccine targeting the 2024-2025 vaccination season and completed FDA biologics license application submission. Novavax plans to initiate a Phase 3 trial for a combination COVID-19-influenza vaccine plus standalone flu vaccine in the second half of 2024, with potential 2026 launch timing. Novavax reduced current liabilities by an additional $831 million during the quarter and removed its going concern notice. The company held $496 million in cash and equivalents as of March 31, 2024.
